summary: Decompiled several functions in src/melee/ft/ft_0892.c, added supporting fighter/IK types and declarations, and improved ft_0892 matching according to the bot report. The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: implements ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, ft_800895E0, fn_8008998C, and ft_80089B08; adds includes, local ftData_x58_t, extern db_804D4AF8, and a PAD_STACK tactic.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ft/ft_0892.c added 360 deleted 8 hunks 3
raw_diff: ft_0892.c replaces placeholder comments for ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, ft_800895E0, fn_8008998C, and ft_80089B08 with C implementations
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports 4 new matches and 3 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/ft/ft_0892

key_files.role: Type support: adds x2085 bitfields to Fighter substructure and defines struct IKState used by the new IK/floor-adjustment code.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ft/types.h added 23 deleted 0 hunks 2
raw_diff: types.h adds x2085_b0 through x2085_b7 bitfields
raw_diff: types.h adds struct IKState with HSD_JObj pointers, Vec3 positions, lengths, and angle fields

key_files.role: Header cleanup: replaces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for fn_8008998C with a concrete s32 fn_8008998C(Fighter*, IKState*, Vec3*) prototype.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ft/ft_0892.h added 1 deleted 1 hunks 1
raw_diff: ft_0892.h changes fn_8008998C prototype from UNK_RET fn_8008998C(UNK_PARAMS) to s32 fn_8008998C(Fighter*, IKState*, Vec3*)

key_files.role: Forward declaration support for the recovered IKState type.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ft/forward.h added 1 deleted 0 hunks 1
raw_diff: forward.h adds typedef struct IKState IKState

what_changed.text: Implemented ft_800892D4 and ft_80089460 to initialize/reset fighter damage and x2070/x2074 state fields, including x2084/x2085 bitfields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_800892D4 body initializes dmg.x18d4, x2070, x2074 vector/flags, dmg.x18d8, x18ec_instancehitby, and x2088
raw_diff: ft_80089460 body clears x2074 state and derives flags from dmg.x1830_percent, is_metal, x197C, x221D_b6, x2226_b4, x2220_b5, and x2220_b6

what_changed.text: Implemented ft_800895E0 as a near-match/improvement that updates x2070 state, handles Luigi and parasol/item special cases, calls ft_80089768, and refreshes damage/status flags.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_800895E0 uses union Struct2070 values, FTKIND_LUIGI, it_8026B6C8, plAttack_80037B08, and ft_80089768
text_corpus: bot reports ft_800895E0 improved from 0.00% to 98.86%

what_changed.text: Implemented fn_8008998C as a matched helper using IKState, floor queries, HSD_JObjGetTranslation2, ABS, slope limiting, and Vec3 normal output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8008998C body calls HSD_JObjGetTranslation2, mpLib_8004DD90_Floor, mpFloorGetLeft, mpFloorGetRight, and uses ABS(delta_y)
text_corpus: bot reports fn_8008998C as a new 100.00% match

what_changed.text: Implemented most of ft_80089B08, covering grounded IK leg adjustment, bg flash helper calls, joint rotation save/restore, floor-line angle computation, clamping, and ftPartSetRotX.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_80089B08 uses IKState, db_804D4AF8, ft_data->x58, lbVector helpers, lbBgFlash helpers, mpLine helpers, atan2f, sqrtf, ABS, and ftPartSetRotX
text_corpus: bot reports ft_80089B08 improved from 0.00% to 97.81%

what_changed.text: Added concrete type and prototype surfaces for IKState and fn_8008998C instead of leaving unknown prototype macros.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h adds struct IKState
raw_diff: forward.h adds typedef struct IKState IKState
raw_diff: ft_0892.h replaces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for fn_8008998C

smart_moves.text: The PR paired body recovery with header/type updates, so the recovered fn_8008998C signature is visible through ft_0892.h and IKState is forward-declared.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_0892.h concrete fn_8008998C prototype
raw_diff: forward.h IKState typedef
raw_diff: types.h struct IKState definition

smart_moves.text: The IK helper was represented with a named struct containing jobj pointers, positions, lengths, and angle limits rather than only raw stack or pointer-offset accesses.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h struct IKState fields
raw_diff: fn_8008998C and ft_80089B08 use IKState* and IKState local

smart_moves.text: Common math/control-flow macros and library helpers were used where visible, including ABS, M_PI_2_F, vector helpers, mpLib/mpLine helpers, and HSD_JObjGetTranslation2.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function signature introduces a new shared struct, update both the owning header prototype and forward declarations in the same PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_0892.h changes fn_8008998C to s32 fn_8008998C(Fighter*, IKState*, Vec3*)
raw_diff: forward.h adds typedef struct IKState IKState
raw_diff: types.h adds struct IKState

decomp_lessons.lesson: A temporary local data-view struct can make repeated ft_data offset accesses reviewable when the underlying ft_data layout is not fully named yet.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_0892.c adds local typedef struct ftData_x58_t with offsets x0, x1, x4, x8, x9, xC, x10, x11, x18
raw_diff: ft_80089B08 casts fp->ft_data->x58 to ftData_x58_t for joint indices and lengths

decomp_lessons.lesson: Bot-reported partial matches can coexist with visible matching tactics; keep the tactic narrow and leave evidence for curator review rather than treating it as general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_80089B08 ends with PAD_STACK(16)
text_corpus: bot reports ft_80089B08 improved to 97.81%, not 100.00%

naming_conventions.lesson: The PR names the recovered inverse-kinematics state struct semantically while its individual fields remain conservative role/offset-style names such as jobj0, pos0, len0, and angle_limit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h defines struct IKState
raw_diff: IKState fields include jobj0, jobj1, jobj2, pos0 through pos4, len0, len1, angle_max, angle_limit

naming_conventions.lesson: For uncertain fighter data sublayouts, the PR uses an address/offset-derived local type name and preserves field-offset names instead of inventing full semantics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_0892.c adds typedef struct ftData_x58_t
raw_diff: ftData_x58_t fields are x0, x1, pad_02, x4, x8, x9, pad_0A, xC, x10, x11, pad_12, x18

naming_conventions.lesson: Newly exposed fighter bitfields stayed offset/bit-index named where semantics were not proven by the slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h adds x2085_b0 through x2085_b7

raw_diff: types.h adds x2085_b0 through x2085_b7 bitfields

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use typed punning/casts through union Struct2070 and pointer casts to force exact state-field stores.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_800892D4 assigns dmg.x18d4 from *(UnkPlBonusBits*) &spC and x2070 from *(s32*) &arg0->dmg.x18d4
raw_diff: ft_800895E0 uses union Struct2070 temporaries spC, val, sp18, and sp14
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result: Contributed to matching or near-matching state update functions, but the PR itself leaves a TODO questioning whether arg1 should be Struct2070.

raw_diff: ft_80089B08 declares u8 _[8]

source_update_candidates.title: Consider noting that stack padding in partial matches needs explicit evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2401 includes PAD_STACK(16) and unnamed u8 padding arrays in matched/partial-matched code. The loaded standards already cover matching tactics needing evidence; this PR can serve as an example candidate, not a new rule.

source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate example for existing matching-tactics standard; not promoted automatically because no author/reviewer explanation is present.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer approval of PAD_STACK or padding arrays; the slice has no review comments.
Do not claim ft_800895E0 or ft_80089B08 fully matched; the bot report lists them as 98.86% and 97.81% improvements respectively.
Do not promote the TODO 'is arg1 not just a Struct2070?' into a fact about the true signature; it is explicitly uncertain in the diff.
Do not infer semantics for ftData_x58_t fields or x2085 bitfields beyond the offset-style names shown in the diff.
